description: Every feature starts as false. Coding agent sets passes=true only after verifying end-to-end. Never delete or edit feature descriptions.

features.description: agentiq.trace() accepts session_id, step_number, step_name, input, output, tool_calls, metadata
trace() is async and fire-and-forget — returns in under 5ms
trace() never raises an exception — fails silently and logs locally
trace() keeps agent running when AgentIQ API is down
POST /trace webhook receiver accepts same payload as SDK
AgentLog table exists with all required fields
SessionSummary table exists with all required fields
EvalResult table exists with quality_score and dimension scores
LossPattern table exists with pct_of_all_failures and root_cause
AgentQualityConfig table exists for per-agent weight configuration
Alembic migrations run cleanly from scratch
Intent classifier classifies billing_dispute intent correctly
Intent classifier handles all 5 intent types
session_builder creates SessionSummary on session_ended=True
session_builder sets correct drop_off_step for incomplete sessions
session_builder builds correct workflow_path string
session_builder is idempotent — running twice creates one row
LLM judge evaluates every AgentLog row and creates EvalResult
quality_score is between 0.0 and 1.0 for all interactions
Known good interaction scores above 0.7
Known bad interaction scores below 0.7
tool_failure correctly classified for billing dispute with API timeout
goal_drift correctly classified — task done but goal unmet
loop correctly classified — agent repeats same output
context_loss correctly classified — agent contradicts prior step
All 7 failure types classified with accuracy above 85%
Judge includes last 3 steps as prior context on every evaluation
Healthcare industry weights: accuracy=0.5, safety=0.3, completeness=0.1, decision_quality=0.1
Coding industry weights: correctness=0.5, completeness=0.3, decision_quality=0.2
Developer can override weights via API — scores recalculate correctly
Malformed judge output triggers one retry then sets eval_error=True
Eval background job is idempotent
Intent pattern detected when 5+ failures share same intent
Workflow step pattern detected when 5+ failures at same step
Tool call pattern detected when tool failure rate exceeds 25%
pct_of_all_failures calculated correctly
root_cause is one specific actionable plain English sentence
Pattern below min_failures threshold not created
is_worsening flag correctly set for increasing failure rates
Pattern analyzer is idempotent
Pattern export JSON matches published schema exactly
POST /trace returns 201 and creates AgentLog
POST /trace returns 422 for missing required fields
GET /agents/{id}/patterns returns ranked loss patterns
GET /agents/{id}/patterns returns empty array (not 404) for no data
Missing API key returns 401
Wrong agent_id returns 403 — multi-tenant isolation enforced
Cursor-based pagination works correctly
raw_judge_output never appears in customer-facing API responses
GET /patterns/{id}/export returns structured eval data JSON
Agent Overview shows quality score trend
Agent Overview shows active failure count
Agent Overview shows top failure types
Failure Feed shows new failures within 30 seconds
Failure Feed groups repeating patterns automatically with root cause
Interaction Detail shows step-by-step trace with per-step quality score
Interaction Detail shows failure reason in plain English sentence
Developer can adjust quality dimension weights in dashboard
Dashboard loads with 500 demo sessions on Railway
seed_data.py generates 500 realistic sessions
Sessions cover all 5 intent types
Sessions include deliberate failures for all 7 failure types
Billing dispute pattern has pct_of_all_failures above 40%
API deploys to Railway and is publicly accessible
Dashboard deploys to Railway and is publicly accessible
Environment variables set correctly — no secrets in repo
Full integration test passes on deployed version
